# OpenPath Design System — Semantic Specification
> Version: 0.2 | For AI context use only. Raw values (hex, px) are intentionally omitted.

---

## Token Architecture

Tokens follow a 3-tier hierarchy. Components MUST only reference Tier 2 (alias) or Tier 3 (component) tokens.
Import path: `../../tokens` (never from `tokens/primitives` inside components).

```
Tier 1 (Primitive)  →  Tier 2 (Alias)  →  Tier 3 (Component)
  raw values              semantic roles       component-specific
  AI context excluded     ← use these          ← or these
```

---

## Alias Tokens

### brand
| Token | Role | When NOT to use |
|---|---|---|
| `alias.brand.primary` | Primary CTA, active states, key brand moments | Decorative elements, icons that aren't interactive |
| `alias.brand.hover` | Hover state for primary interactive elements | Static text or non-interactive surfaces |
| `alias.brand.pressed` | Pressed/active state | Default/resting states |
| `alias.brand.light` | Tinted backgrounds, selected state fills | Primary text — contrast is insufficient |

### text
| Token | Role | When NOT to use |
|---|---|---|
| `alias.text.primary` | Main body copy, headings | Placeholder or helper text |
| `alias.text.secondary` | Supporting text, labels, metadata | Headings or primary content |
| `alias.text.disabled` | Placeholder, inactive elements | Active/enabled elements |
| `alias.text.inverse` | Text on dark or brand-colored surfaces | Light backgrounds |

### surface
| Token | Role | When NOT to use |
|---|---|---|
| `alias.surface.base` | Card/panel/modal backgrounds | Page-level backgrounds |
| `alias.surface.subtle` | Page background, section dividers | Cards or panels that need to stand out |
| `alias.surface.inverse` | Snackbar, Tooltip backgrounds | Large content areas |
| `alias.surface.overlay` | Modal/drawer backdrop | Anything other than overlays |

### border
| Token | Role |
|---|---|
| `alias.border.default` | Default input, card, divider strokes |
| `alias.border.focus` | Focused input ring |
| `alias.border.error` | Error state ring |

### feedback
| Token | Role | When NOT to use |
|---|---|---|
| `alias.feedback.success` | Positive outcomes, completion | Neutral informational states |
| `alias.feedback.warning` | Caution, irreversible actions | Errors that need immediate correction |
| `alias.feedback.error` | Destructive actions, validation failures | Warnings or soft cautions |
| `alias.feedback.info` | Informational, neutral system messages | Feedback tied to user action outcome |

---

## Component Token Rules

Styling for each component maps to CSS custom properties via `variables.css`. The JS token references below reflect the semantic intent; in CSS Modules use the corresponding `--var-name` from `variables.css`.

### Button
- Background (filled): `component.button.primaryBg` → `--btn-primary-bg`
- Hover: `component.button.primaryHover` → `--btn-primary-hover`
- Pressed: `component.button.primaryPressed` → `--btn-primary-pressed`
- Outlined border: `component.button.secondaryBorder` → `--color-brand-primary`
- Elevation: none (buttons are flat)

### Input
- Default border: `component.input.border` → `--input-border`
- Focus ring: `component.input.focusBorder` → `--input-focus-border`
- Error ring: `component.input.errorBorder` → `--input-error-border`
- Disabled background: `component.input.disabledBg` → `--input-disabled-bg`
- Elevation: none

### Modal
- Background: `alias.surface.base` → `--color-surface-base`
- Backdrop: `alias.surface.overlay` → `--color-surface-overlay`
- Elevation: `elevation[4]` → `--elevation-4`
- Border-radius: `shape.xl` → `--shape-xl`

### Card
- elevated: `alias.surface.base` + `elevation[2]` → `--elevation-2`
- outlined: `alias.surface.base` + `alias.border.default` (1px border)
- filled: `alias.surface.subtle`, no shadow
- Border-radius: `shape.lg` → `--shape-lg`

### AppBar
- Background: `alias.surface.base`
- Default shadow: `elevation[1]` → `--elevation-1`
- Elevated shadow: `elevation[2]` → `--elevation-2`
- Height: fixed 56px

### TabBar
- Active indicator: `alias.brand.primary` (2px bottom border)
- Active text: `alias.brand.primary`, weight 600
- Inactive text: `alias.text.secondary`, weight 400
- Elevation: none (sits on surface)

### FAB
- Background: `alias.brand.primary`
- Hover: `alias.brand.hover`
- Elevation: `elevation[3]` → `--elevation-3` (resting), `elevation[4]` on hover
- sm → `shape.md`, md → `shape.lg`, lg → `shape.xl`, extended → `shape.full`

### Snackbar
- default: `alias.surface.inverse` background
- success/error/info: corresponding `alias.feedback.*`
- Text: `alias.text.inverse`
- Elevation: `elevation[3]` → `--elevation-3`
- Border-radius: `shape.md` → `--shape-md`

### List / ListItem
- Container: `alias.surface.base`, `alias.border.default` (1px border), `shape.md`
- Divider: `alias.border.default` (1px bottom border on each item)
- Hover (clickable): `alias.surface.subtle`
- Elevation: none

---

## Typography Scale

Use `typography.scale.<name>` — each entry has `.size`, `.weight`, `.lineHeight`.
`overline` also has `.letterSpacing` and `.textTransform: 'uppercase'` — apply both when using it.

| Name | Use case | When NOT to use |
|---|---|---|
| `display1`, `display2` | Hero / landing headlines | Body content, cards |
| `heading1` | Page-level titles | Sub-sections inside cards |
| `heading2` | Section titles | Single-line labels |
| `heading3` | Card titles, drawer headers | Page titles |
| `body1` | Primary reading content | Labels, captions |
| `body2` | Secondary content, descriptions | Headings |
| `label` | Button text, form labels, tab items | Long-form reading content |
| `caption` | Helper text, timestamps, metadata | Primary content |
| `overline` | Section headers above lists — apply `textTransform: uppercase` and `letterSpacing` | Paragraph text |

Font family: `typography.fontFamily.sans` (all UI), `typography.fontFamily.mono` (code, token values only)

---

## Spacing Scale

`spacing[n]` — 4px base grid. Returns a CSS string (e.g. `spacing[4]` = `'16px'`).

| Key | Value | Common use |
|---|---|---|
| `spacing[1]` | 4px | Icon-to-label gap, tight padding |
| `spacing[2]` | 8px | Chip/badge padding, tight gaps |
| `spacing[3]` | 12px | Small component inner padding |
| `spacing[4]` | 16px | Default padding, form field gaps |
| `spacing[5]` | 20px | Medium padding, compact card sections |
| `spacing[6]` | 24px | Card padding, comfortable gaps |
| `spacing[8]` | 32px | Section separation |
| `spacing[10]` | 40px | Page-level vertical rhythm |

Do not use arbitrary px values. All spacing must reference `spacing[n]`.

---

## Shape Scale

`shape.<name>` — border-radius steps.

| Name | Value | Use |
|---|---|---|
| `shape.none` | 0px | Dividers, full-bleed elements |
| `shape.xs` | 4px | Badges, tooltips |
| `shape.sm` | 8px | Inputs, small cards |
| `shape.md` | 12px | Modals, menus, Snackbar |
| `shape.lg` | 16px | Cards, drawers, FAB (md) |
| `shape.xl` | 28px | Bottom sheets, large modals, FAB (lg) |
| `shape.full` | 9999px | Buttons (pill), Chips, FAB (extended) |

---

## Elevation Scale

`elevation[0–5]` — box-shadow depth. Use the lowest level that achieves necessary separation.

| Level | Component mapping |
|---|---|
| `elevation[0]` | Flat surfaces — no separation needed |
| `elevation[1]` | AppBar (default) |
| `elevation[2]` | Card (elevated variant), AppBar (elevated prop) |
| `elevation[3]` | Snackbar, FAB (resting) |
| `elevation[4]` | Modal, Drawer, FAB (hover) |
| `elevation[5]` | Reserved for urgent overlays |

---

## Components

---

### Button

**Props**
| Prop | Type | Default | Valid values |
|---|---|---|---|
| `variant` | string | `'filled'` | `'filled'` `'outlined'` `'tonal'` `'ghost'` |
| `size` | string | `'md'` | `'sm'` `'md'` `'lg'` |
| `disabled` | boolean | `false` | — |
| `fullWidth` | boolean | `false` | — |
| `leftIcon` | ReactNode | — | Any inline element |
| `rightIcon` | ReactNode | — | Any inline element |
| `onClick` | function | — | — |

**When to use each variant**
- `filled` — The single primary action on a screen (submit, confirm, proceed)
- `outlined` — Secondary action alongside a filled button (cancel, back)
- `tonal` — Soft emphasis without full brand weight (filter toggles, secondary CTAs)
- `ghost` — Inline or tertiary action where visual weight would clutter (edit, delete link)

**When NOT to use**
- Do not place two `filled` buttons side by side — only one primary action per context
- Do not use `ghost` as the sole action — it lacks enough affordance when used alone
- Do not use `size="lg"` inside compact UI like cards or list rows

**Examples**
```jsx
<Button variant="filled" onClick={handleSubmit}>저장하기</Button>
<Button variant="outlined" onClick={handleCancel}>취소</Button>
<Button variant="filled" leftIcon="＋">항목 추가</Button>
<Button variant="filled" fullWidth>로그인</Button>
```

---

### Input

**Props**
| Prop | Type | Default | Valid values |
|---|---|---|---|
| `label` | string | — | — |
| `placeholder` | string | — | — |
| `value` | string | — | — |
| `onChange` | function | — | — |
| `helperText` | string | — | Guidance shown below input |
| `error` | string | — | Replaces helperText when set |
| `disabled` | boolean | `false` | — |
| `type` | string | `'text'` | Any valid HTML input type |

**When NOT to use**
- Do not omit `label` — always provide one for accessibility
- Do not pass both `helperText` and `error` — `error` takes visual priority and `helperText` is hidden
- Do not use for multi-line text — use a `textarea` instead

**Examples**
```jsx
<Input label="이름" placeholder="홍길동" value={name} onChange={e => setName(e.target.value)} />
<Input label="이메일" error="올바른 이메일 형식이 아닙니다" value={email} onChange={e => setEmail(e.target.value)} />
<Input label="비밀번호" type="password" helperText="8자 이상, 특수문자 포함" />
```

---

### Modal

**Props**
| Prop | Type | Default | Valid values |
|---|---|---|---|
| `open` | boolean | — | — |
| `title` | string | — | — |
| `description` | string | — | — |
| `onConfirm` | function | — | — |
| `onCancel` | function | — | — |
| `confirmLabel` | string | `'확인'` | — |
| `cancelLabel` | string | `'취소'` | — |

**When NOT to use**
- Do not use for non-critical notifications — use Snackbar instead
- Do not nest Modals
- Do not omit `onCancel` — users must always have an escape path

**Examples**
```jsx
<Modal
  open={isOpen}
  title="정말 삭제하시겠어요?"
  description="이 작업은 되돌릴 수 없습니다."
  confirmLabel="삭제"
  cancelLabel="취소"
  onConfirm={handleDelete}
  onCancel={() => setIsOpen(false)}
/>
```

---

### Chips

**Props**
| Prop | Type | Default | Valid values |
|---|---|---|---|
| `label` | string | — | — |
| `selected` | boolean | `false` | — |
| `onClick` | function | — | Filter/toggle handler |
| `onClose` | function | — | Makes chip removable |
| `disabled` | boolean | `false` | — |

**When NOT to use**
- Do not use chips as primary navigation — use TabBar instead
- Do not use `onClose` and `onClick` for conflicting actions on the same chip

**Examples**
```jsx
<Chips label="React" selected={selected} onClick={() => toggle('React')} />
<Chips label="디자인시스템" selected onClose={() => removeTag('디자인시스템')} />
```

---

### Card

**Props**
| Prop | Type | Default | Valid values |
|---|---|---|---|
| `variant` | string | `'elevated'` | `'elevated'` `'outlined'` `'filled'` |
| `padding` | string | `'24px'` | Any CSS string — use `spacing[n]` values (e.g. `spacing[4]` = `'16px'`) |
| `style` | object | — | Additional inline styles |

**When to use each variant**
- `elevated` — Standalone content cards (`elevation[2]` shadow)
- `outlined` — Form sections, grouped inputs, structured data panels (1px border, no shadow)
- `filled` — Subtle background grouping, secondary content blocks (no border, no shadow)

**When NOT to use**
- Do not nest `elevated` cards — use `outlined` or `filled` for inner groupings
- Do not use Card as a button wrapper — use a button with appropriate styling

**Examples**
```jsx
<Card variant="elevated">
  <h3>제목</h3>
  <p>내용</p>
  <Button size="sm" variant="outlined">자세히</Button>
</Card>

// compact form section
<Card variant="outlined" padding={spacing[5]}>
  <Input label="이름" />
  <Input label="이메일" />
</Card>
```

---

### AppBar

**Props**
| Prop | Type | Default | Valid values |
|---|---|---|---|
| `title` | string | — | — |
| `leading` | ReactNode | — | Back icon, menu icon, or any element |
| `trailing` | ReactNode | — | Icon buttons, action group |
| `elevated` | boolean | `false` | Switches from `elevation[1]` to `elevation[2]` |

**When NOT to use**
- Do not put more than 2–3 trailing actions — use a menu overflow instead
- Do not use as a section header inside a page — use `heading2`/`heading3` typography

**Examples**
```jsx
// String shorthand (acceptable for prototyping)
<AppBar title="설정" leading="←" trailing={<>⋮</>} />

// ReactNode (recommended for production)
<AppBar title="홈" leading={<BackButton />} trailing={<IconButton icon="search" />} elevated />
```

---

### TabBar

**Import:** `import { TabBar } from '../../components/Tab'`

**Props**
| Prop | Type | Default | Valid values |
|---|---|---|---|
| `items` | string[] | — | 2–5 items |
| `activeIndex` | number | — | — |
| `onChange` | function | — | — |

**When NOT to use**
- Do not use more than 5 tabs — consider a dropdown or navigation drawer
- Do not use for sequential steps — use a stepper pattern instead

**Examples**
```jsx
const [tab, setTab] = useState(0);
<TabBar items={['홈', '탐색', '활동', '프로필']} activeIndex={tab} onChange={setTab} />
```

---

### FAB

**Props**
| Prop | Type | Default | Valid values |
|---|---|---|---|
| `icon` | ReactNode | — | — |
| `label` | string | — | Required when `size="extended"` |
| `size` | string | `'md'` | `'sm'` `'md'` `'lg'` `'extended'` |
| `onClick` | function | — | — |

**When NOT to use**
- Do not use more than one FAB per screen
- Do not use FAB for destructive actions
- Do not use `sm` FAB as the primary action — touch target is too small

**Examples**
```jsx
<FAB icon="＋" size="md" onClick={handleCreate} />
<FAB icon="✏" label="작성하기" size="extended" onClick={handleWrite} />
```

---

### Snackbar

**Props**
| Prop | Type | Default | Valid values |
|---|---|---|---|
| `open` | boolean | — | — |
| `message` | string | — | — |
| `type` | string | `'default'` | `'default'` `'success'` `'error'` `'info'` |
| `action` | `{ label: string, onClick: function }` | — | Single optional action |
| `onClose` | function | — | Called on auto-dismiss or action |
| `duration` | number \| null | `3000` | ms — pass `null` to disable auto-close |

**`duration: null` behaviour:** auto-close timer is disabled. The snackbar stays open until `onClose` is triggered externally (e.g. via `action.onClick`). When using `duration={null}`, always provide an `action` — otherwise the user has no way to dismiss it.

**When NOT to use**
- Do not stack multiple Snackbars — show one at a time
- Do not use for critical errors requiring user action — use Modal instead
- Do not use for content that takes more than ~5 seconds to read

**Examples**
```jsx
// Auto-dismiss
<Snackbar open={saved} message="저장되었습니다" type="success" onClose={() => setSaved(false)} />

// Persistent with undo
<Snackbar
  open={deleted}
  message="항목이 삭제되었습니다"
  duration={null}
  action={{ label: '실행 취소', onClick: handleUndo }}
  onClose={() => setDeleted(false)}
/>
```

---

### List / ListItem

**List Props**
| Prop | Type | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `children` | ReactNode | — |
| `style` | object | — |

**ListItem Props**
| Prop | Type | Default | Valid values |
|---|---|---|---|
| `title` | string | — | — |
| `subtitle` | string | — | — |
| `leading` | ReactNode | — | Icon, avatar |
| `trailing` | ReactNode | — | Icon, badge, metadata |
| `onClick` | function | — | Makes item interactive (adds hover state) |
| `divider` | boolean | `true` | Set `false` on the last item |

**When NOT to use**
- Do not use List for fewer than 2 items
- Do not put complex interactive controls (inputs, buttons) inside ListItem
- Always set `divider={false}` on the last ListItem to avoid a double border

**Examples**
```jsx
<List>
  <ListItem leading="◈" title="프로필" subtitle="계정 정보 관리" trailing="›" onClick={goToProfile} />
  <ListItem leading="🔔" title="알림" trailing="›" onClick={goToNotifs} />
  <ListItem leading="⚙" title="설정" trailing="›" onClick={goToSettings} divider={false} />
</List>
```

---

## Design Principles

**Clarity** — Minimize cognitive load. Predictable patterns, clear hierarchy.
**Scalability** — Token-based: brand/theme changes propagate without touching components.
**Autonomy** — Each component is self-contained. No external state required to render.
